title: PTD Today — Media

items.title: Would you go to an in-person dating app event?

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Hinge announced a $1 million fund for social groups in New York, Los Angeles and London to support free or affordable social events for young people as a global expansion of its One More Hour initiative. The effort is presented as a response to rising loneliness among Gen Z.
bullets: Hinge launched a $1M fund for social groups.
Fund targets New York, Los Angeles and London.
Grants support free or affordable social events for young people.
Part of Hinge’s One More Hour social impact initiative.
Framed as addressing increasing loneliness among Gen Z.
takeaways: No direct grid or data center infrastructure impacts are described in the available description.
Events are concentrated in major urban centers (NY, LA, London); operators may want to monitor localized event activity for potential short-term increases in venue energy demand.
If such events scale or become recurring, coordination with venue operators and local utilities could help anticipate reliability or capacity needs.
Track similar large-platform social initiatives as possible indicators of changing urban event patterns that could affect local energy usage.

items.title: Build Knowledge Graph-powered AI Agents For Your Use Case | Databases for AI

ai.summary: Based on the available description… Knowledge graphs are presented as the fix for AI agents that lack company-specific understanding. The session demonstrates building a knowledge graph automatically from raw documents and connecting it to an agent that understands your business, with no graph expertise required and an end-to-end build; it also links to a prior episode comparing VectorDB vs GraphDB for Gen AI agents.
bullets: Knowledge graphs can give AI agents company-specific knowledge they currently lack.
Session shows automated construction of a knowledge graph from raw documents.
Demonstrates connecting the graph to an AI agent so it understands your business.
No graph theory or query expertise required for the workflow shown.
References a related episode comparing VectorDB and GraphDB for Gen AI agents.
takeaways: Turn internal documents into a knowledge graph so AI agents can answer org-specific questions.
A no-expertise-required approach lowers the barrier for operational teams to adopt graph-powered agents.
An end-to-end demo provides a practical starting point to evaluate for infrastructure, ops, or data-center use cases.
The linked episode on VectorDB vs GraphDB can help inform database choices for deployment.

items.title: Leveraging tax credits and incentives for sustainability projects

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Randa Barsoum of PwC US highlights tax credits and incentives for sustainability that go beyond the usual energy-focused programs. She calls out state and local abatements, location-based incentives, and R&D tax credits tied to innovation (for example, reimagining packaging or processes), and emphasizes that a broader view of tax opportunities can unlock meaningful value from sustainability investments.
bullets: Energy incentives are often top of mind, but not the whole picture.
State and local abatements can support sustainability projects.
Location-based incentives may influence project siting and economics.
R&D tax credits can apply to innovation such as packaging or process redesigns.
Viewing sustainability investments through a broader tax lens can unlock meaningful value.
takeaways: Don’t limit tax planning to energy credits — evaluate state/local abatements and location-based incentives when siting or expanding facilities.
Look for R&D credit opportunities tied to process or design innovations that improve sustainability or efficiency.
Include broader tax incentives in ROI and site-selection analyses for grid/data center projects to capture additional value.
Engage tax expertise early to identify non-obvious credits and integrate them into project planning.

ai.summary: Based on the available description, the sky is full of stars and Copilot helps Christa find the star within. The short promo invites viewers to download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
bullets: Short, inspirational promo using a star metaphor featuring Christa.
Positions Copilot as a tool that helps users discover their potential.
Clear call-to-action: download and try Copilot via the link in the bio.
No transcript available for the video.
takeaways: Based on the description, Copilot is framed as a user-facing assistant—decision-makers can download and try it to assess its user support capabilities.
Based on the description, the clear CTA enables quick hands-on evaluation—consider using the link to run a short pilot with staff.
Based on the description, messaging emphasizes personal empowerment—assess whether that tone aligns with employee engagement goals during any trial.

items.title: Bringing In $251K A Year With 3 Jobs In The Bay Area

ai.summary: Based on the available description, Naseema McElroy is a 44-year-old labor and delivery nurse in Brentwood, California who brings in $251K a year by working three jobs and running a personal finance brand. She overcame nearly $1 million in debt starting in 2015, launched Financially Intentional to document her payoff journey, and was debt-free by 2017; the piece is part of CNBC Make It's Millennial Money series.
bullets: Naseema McElroy, 44, is a labor and delivery nurse and personal finance creator based in Brentwood, CA.
She earns $251,000 annually by holding three jobs in the Bay Area.
In 2015 she had nearly $1 million in debt and began learning better money management.
She started the brand Financially Intentional to document her debt payoff journey.
By 2017 she was debt-free; the video is part of CNBC Make It's Millennial Money series.
takeaways: Profiles showing multi-job, high-earning Bay Area residents (e.g., $251K/year across three jobs) signal local demand for reliable residential and commercial power/connectivity infrastructure.
Creator-led small businesses (like Financially Intentional) emphasize the need for dependable digital platforms and hosting services with strong uptime guarantees.
Rapid financial turnaround stories highlight the importance of resilient remote-work capabilities that enable flexible, multiple-income work arrangements.
Local demographic and income profiles from such features can inform capacity planning and service-level expectations for data center and grid operations.

items.title: GeForce Garage - Summer of RTX Community Build

ai.summary: Based on the available description… NVIDIA's GeForce Garage highlights the winner of the Summer of RTX r/buildapc community contest, a small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 build by u/JeffersonPutnam hosted by Dwight. The system uses a Fractal Design Era 2 chassis and is equipped with a GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition, Ryzen 7 9800X3D, 32GB DDR5-6000, Corsair SF1000 PSU, and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
bullets: Winner: u/JeffersonPutnam — small-form-factor GeForce RTX 5090 rig.
Chassis: Fractal Design Era 2; GPU: GeForce RTX 5090 Founders Edition.
CPU: Ryzen 7 9800X3D with 32GB Teamgroup T-Create Expert DDR5-6000.
Power & cooling: Corsair SF1000 SFX power supply and Corsair Nautilus 240 RS AIO.
PCPartPicker system specs and GeForce host/links included in description.
takeaways: Deploying top-tier GPUs like the RTX 5090 in compact enclosures highlights increased power and thermal density considerations for system design.
Use of a 1000W SFX PSU (Corsair SF1000) signals significant per-system power provisioning needs when integrating high-performance GPUs.
Inclusion of a 240mm AIO liquid cooler (Nautilus 240 RS) indicates that robust cooling solutions may be required even in small form-factor builds.
Community showcase builds provide concrete configuration examples useful for compatibility testing and reference when evaluating consumer-facing GPU deployments.

items.title: TTEC accelerates AI deployment and revenue streams with Google Cloud Consulting

ai.summary: Based on the available description, TTEC worked with Google Cloud Consulting to rapidly prototype and deploy GenAI solutions on Google Cloud, enabling new revenue streams while streamlining operations. The engagement boosted developer productivity and compressed TTEC’s digital transformation timeline, expanding the company’s business ambitions.
bullets: TTEC partnered with Google Cloud Consulting to accelerate AI deployment.
GenAI and Google Cloud infrastructure were used to prototype and deploy solutions quickly.
The project unlocked entirely new revenue streams for TTEC.
Developer productivity improved, and operations were streamlined.
Google Cloud Consulting helped compress the digital transformation timeline.
takeaways: Cloud infrastructure and consulting can accelerate AI deployment and shorten time-to-value.
Robust cloud platforms enable faster prototyping that can lead to new revenue opportunities.
Improved developer productivity reduces operational friction during transformation.
Partnering with cloud consultants can expand what’s achievable within digital transformation timelines.
